intent: Standalone exclude-msgtype suppression (catalog #10). Two -a always,exclude -F msgtype=<TYPE> rules that drop whole record types (PROCTITLE, PATH) from the event stream. These are SUPPRESSIVE: they reduce per-event byte volume produced by the additive rules; they never add events. Tests that a cost impl correctly classifies 'always,exclude' as suppressive, not additive.

validation.method: grammar-only (per f3 section 2.4 and generator prompt: containers cannot load audit rules without kernel netlink; VMs needed for live-load; grammar-only is correct tier for this scenario)

rules.note: Drops PROCTITLE sub-records (~100 B each, AUDIT_PROCTITLE=1327). Contributes 0 additive volume; a naive impl that treats 'always,exclude' as 'always,exit' counts this as additive MEDIUM volume (the trap).
Drops PATH sub-records (~200 B each, AUDIT_PATH=1302). Contributes 0 additive volume. Same trap as PROCTITLE exclude.

suspected_trap: Both -a always,exclude rules use action 'always', which a naive impl confuses with 'always,exit' and counts as additive MEDIUM volume. Correct: 'always,exclude' is the exclude filter list, direction=suppressive, contributing 0 additive events and only reducing bytes on events produced by the additive rules below. A naive impl adds two spurious MEDIUM bands (~$2.76/mo each), inflating total from ~$2.76/mo to ~$8.21/mo (3x overstatement). The key assertion: classifying 'always,exclude' as suppressive (direction=suppressive, tier NEGATIVE) regardless of the 'always' action word.
